title: Conflict in Ethiopia (1997 - 2015)
description:
Ethiopia is one of the oldest independent countries in the world, and the largest landlocked country located in the horn of Africa. The Ethiopian Kingdom was founded in the 10th century BC with the kingdom of D’mt,
home to the fabled Queen of Sheba which is recounted in the King James Bible. 
Civil wars have characterized Ethiopia's existence since the 19th century. This was the time when Italy first tried to invade Ethiopia, however, was defeated by Menelik's forces at the Battle of Adwa on 1 March 1896.
Conflicts which took place from 1997 to 2015 were recorded by the Human Rights Council. The major causes of the conflict were disagreements about the possession or use of land, grazing land or water resources, and about settlements, regional hegemony, access to State resources (funds, jobs, investments) and language policy in education and administration (Aalen 2002:70). 
The majority of conflicts in Ethiopia are about land and the geographical boundaries between the territories of different ethnic groups (Abbink 2006:389). This is because the federal structure, which is based on ethnicity, contributes to ethnic tensions and conflicts, widening the disparities among the ethnic groups. These ethnic conflicts then lead to injury, death, and the further displacements of citizens.
stepByStepContent:
Conflict in Ethiopia (1997 - 2015)
Ethiopia is one of the oldest independent countries in the world, and the largest landlocked country located in the horn of Africa. The Ethiopian Kingdom was founded in the 10th century BC with the kingdom of D’mt, home to the fabled Queen of Sheba which is recounted in the King James Bible. Civil wars have characterized Ethiopia's existence since the 19th century. This was the time when Italy first tried to invade Ethiopia, however, was defeated by Menelik's forces at the Battle of Adwa on 1 March 1896. Conflicts which took place from 1997 to 2015 were recorded by the Human Rights Council. The major causes of the conflict were disagreements about the possession or use of land, grazing land or water resources, and about settlements, regional hegemony, access to State resources (funds, jobs, investments) and language policy in education and administration (Aalen 2002:70). The majority of conflicts in Ethiopia are about land and the geographical boundaries between the territories of different ethnic groups (Abbink 2006:389). This is because the federal structure, which is based on ethnicity, contributes to ethnic tensions and conflicts, widening the disparities among the ethnic groups. These ethnic conflicts then lead to injury, death, and the further displacements of citizens.

Conflict in Ethiopia (1997 - 2015)
Ethiopia is one of the oldest independent countries in the world, and the largest landlocked country located in the horn of Africa. The Ethiopian Kingdom was founded in the 10th century BC with the kingdom of D’mt, home to the fabled Queen of Sheba which is recounted in the King James Bible. Civil wars have characterized Ethiopia's existence since the 19th century. This was the time when Italy first tried to invade Ethiopia, however, was defeated by Menelik's forces at the Battle of Adwa on 1 March 1896. Conflicts which took place from 1997 to 2015 were recorded by the Human Rights Council. The major causes of the conflict were disagreements about the possession or use of land, grazing land or water resources, and about settlements, regional hegemony, access to State resources (funds, jobs, investments) and language policy in education and administration (Aalen 2002:70). The majority of conflicts in Ethiopia are about land and the geographical boundaries between the territories of different ethnic groups (Abbink 2006:389). Step Name:Create Date Table and Sort
Detailed Instructions:

Step1

From Power Bi desktop Menu click Modeling then click New Table and then paste and replace 'Table =' by the following Dax expression to create the date table:

Date = 

ADDCOLUMNS (
CALENDAR (Min('MasterXLS'[EVENT_DATE]), MAX(MasterXLS[EVENT_DATE])),
"Dates", FORMAT ( [Date], "MM/DD/YYYY" ),
"DateAsInteger", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYYMMDD" ),
"Year", YEAR ( [Date] ),
"Monthnumber", FORMAT ( [Date], "MM" ),
"YearMonthnumber", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYY/MM" ),
"YearMonthShort", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYY/mmm" ),
"MonthNameShort", FORMAT ( [Date], "mmm" ),
"Month", FORMAT ( [Date], "mmmm" ),
"DayOfWeekNumber", WEEKDAY ( [Date] ),
"DayOfWeek", FORMAT ( [Date], "dddd" ),
"DayOfWeekShort", FORMAT ( [Date], "ddd" ),
"Quarter", "Q" & FORMAT ( [Date], "Q" ),
"YearQuarter", FORMAT ( [Date], "YYYY" ) & "/Q" & FORMAT ( [Date], "Q" ) 

)

Step2

Sort the date Table:
Select Month then 
in column tools select sort by column then 
choose Monthnumber to sort

Insight: 

Creating Calendar Table in Power BI using DAX functions

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_dd6kiYEm70

Sort month name chronologically in Power BI Desktop

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NlX7Cp2P0d4

Ethiopia is one of the oldest independent countries in the world, and the largest landlocked country located in the horn of Africa. The Ethiopian Kingdom was founded in the 10th century BC with the kingdom of D’mt, home to the fabled Queen of Sheba which is recounted in the King James Bible. Civil wars have characterized Ethiopia's existence since the 19th century. This was the time when Italy first tried to invade Ethiopia, however, was defeated by Menelik's forces at the Battle of Adwa on 1 March 1896. Conflicts which took place from 1997 to 2015 were recorded by the Human Rights Council. The major causes of the conflict were disagreements about the possession or use of land, grazing land or water resources, and about settlements, regional hegemony, access to State resources (funds, jobs, investments) and language policy in education and administration (Aalen 2002:70). Step Name:Creating Dax
Detailed Instructions:

To Create a DAX Measure, follow these steps:

·        Select the table “ AllMeasures”

·     Right Click and select New Measure

·      Write the following DAX Measures on the space provide one at a

time and save

Deaths per Mth = DIVIDE([TotalDeath],DISTINCTCOUNT('Date'[YearMonthnumber]),0) + 0

%MOM =

VAR TotalDeathLM =

    CALCULATE([TotalDeath], DATEADD('Date'[Date],-1,MONTH))

RETURN

DIVIDE(([TotalDeath]-TotalDeathLM),TotalDeathLM)

  

%YOY =

VAR TotalDeathLY =

    CALCULATE([TotalDeath], DATEADD('Date'[Date],-1,YEAR))

RETURN

DIVIDE([TotalDeath]-TotalDeathLY,TotalDeathLY)

 

TotalDeath = SUM(MasterXLS[FATALITIES])

 

TotalDeath_LM = CALCULATE([TotalDeath], DATEADD('Date'[Date],-1, MONTH))

 

TotalDeath_LY = CALCULATE(SUM(MasterXLS[FATALITIES]), DATEADD('Date'[Date],-1, YEAR))

 

The Following gif will guide you on how to create the measures:

 

Follow the same procedure to insert the remaining five Dax Measures

Insight: 

How to use Power BI DAX - Tutorial

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=waG_JhBgUpM&t=3s

Adding Project Title

Adding Year Slicer

Steps4

Step4a: create Refresh Date

How to create Refresh Date:

           Transform data to Power Query.

2.       2.     Click New sources on left corner

3.       3.     Click Blank Query

4.      4.       Enter =DateTime.LocalNow() & press enter

5.       5.      Rename Queryn1 by Date Refresh

6.       6.      Convert to Table Format

7.       7.       Rename Column1 by DateTime

8.       8.        Go to Transform and Date choose Date only

9.       9.         Duplicate DateTime and rename it Time and under Time choose Time only

Conflict in Ethiopia (1997 - 2015)
Ethiopia is one of the oldest independent countries in the world, and the largest landlocked country located in the horn of Africa. The Ethiopian Kingdom was founded in the 10th century BC with the kingdom of D’mt, home to the fabled Queen of Sheba which is recounted in the King James Bible. Civil wars have characterized Ethiopia's existence since the 19th century. This was the time when Italy first tried to invade Ethiopia, however, was defeated by Menelik's forces at the Battle of Adwa on 1 March 1896. Conflicts which took place from 1997 to 2015 were recorded by the Human Rights Council. The major causes of the conflict were disagreements about the possession or use of land, grazing land or water resources, and about settlements, regional hegemony, access to State resources (funds, jobs, investments) and language policy in education and administration (Aalen 2002:70). The majority of conflicts in Ethiopia are about land and the geographical boundaries between the territories of different ethnic groups (Abbink 2006:389). This is because the federal structure, which is based on ethnicity, contributes to ethnic tensions and conflicts, widening the disparities among the ethnic groups. These ethnic conflicts then lead to injury, death, and the further displacements of citizens.

Did you complete the Project?

portfolioContent.title: Conflict in Ethiopia (1997 - 2015)
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Analyzed Ethiopia's ethnic conflicts (1997-2015) using data visualization to uncover causes and trends impacting regional stability.
portfolioContent.summary: This project analyzed recorded conflicts in Ethiopia from 1997 to 2015, focusing on ethnic tensions and resource disputes. Using Power BI, it visualized deaths and conflict trends by interaction groups to identify key conflict drivers. The analysis highlighted how federal ethnic-based structures contribute to ongoing disputes.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Ethiopia faces persistent ethnic conflicts driven by disputes over land, resources, and political power, resulting in injuries, deaths, and displacement. Understanding these conflicts is critical for policymakers to address root causes and improve regional stability. However, fragmented data and complex ethnic dynamics hinder effective conflict resolution.
objectives: Visualize conflict data to identify patterns and trends over time
Analyze causes of ethnic conflicts related to land and resource disputes
Provide actionable insights to support government policy and conflict mitigation
workflow: Collect and preprocess conflict data from Human Rights Council records
Identify key variables such as deaths, interaction groups, and time periods
Create line and stacked column charts in Power BI to visualize deaths and % changes
Format visuals for clarity including legends, axes, and data labels
Filter data by interaction groups to analyze specific conflict drivers
keyInsights: Most conflicts stem from disputes over land and geographical boundaries between ethnic groups
Federal ethnic-based governance structure exacerbates tensions and disparities
Access to state resources and language policies are significant conflict triggers
Conflict trends show fluctuations in deaths with notable year-over-year and month-over-month changes
businessImpact: Informed government stakeholders on ethnic conflict patterns to aid policy formulation
Enhanced understanding of conflict drivers to support targeted interventions
Provided a replicable analytics framework for monitoring future conflict data
